growth_records.agit: varies: spinning on a wheel (hydrogen/shaking conditions) vs static/no shaking (nitrogen conditions); proteomics cultures shaken at room temperature
growth_records.medium: RS-1 growth medium (RGM), gassed with 10% hydrogen balanced with nitrogen
growth_records.cite: Russell et al. A network of coiled-coil and actin-like proteins controls the cellular organization of magnetosome organelles in deep-branching magnetotactic bacteria. Nature Communications (2025). DOI:10.1038/s41467-025-66326-2. [PMC12748827]
growth_records.snippet: growth measurements showed similar final cell densities in hydrogen and nitrogen (Fig. 2D), with hydrogen-grown cells exhibiting faster doubling times (6 vs. 8 hours)
growth_records.notes: Doubling time of 6 h explicitly reported (mean of n=3 biological replicates) for D. magneticus RS-1 grown anaerobically at 30C in RGM medium gassed with 10% H2 (balance N2) in the headspace and medium. mu = ln2/6h = 0.1155/h.

growth_records.agit: varies: spinning on a wheel (hydrogen/shaking conditions) vs static/no shaking (nitrogen conditions); proteomics cultures shaken at room temperature
growth_records.cite: Russell et al. A network of coiled-coil and actin-like proteins controls the cellular organization of magnetosome organelles in deep-branching magnetotactic bacteria. Nature Communications (2025). DOI:10.1038/s41467-025-66326-2. [PMC12748827]
growth_records.snippet: growth measurements showed similar final cell densities in hydrogen and nitrogen (Fig. 2D), with hydrogen-grown cells exhibiting faster doubling times (6 vs. 8 hours)
growth_records.notes: Doubling time of 8 h explicitly reported (mean of n=3 biological replicates) for D. magneticus RS-1 grown anaerobically at 30C in RGM medium with nitrogen headspace (no added H2). mu = ln2/8h = 0.0866/h.
